Evaluation
How will you evaluate your project’s success? Which activities will you carry out in order to assess whether, and to what
extent, your project has reached its objectives and results?
At the start, we will conduct a baseline assessment to evaluate participants' existing knowledge, skills, and attitudes
regarding misinformation, media literacy, digital ethics, and critical thinking. This pre-assessment survey will include a mix of
multiple-choice questions, self-assessment scales, and qualitative responses to measure participants' familiarity with
misinformation strategies, fact-checking techniques, and digital responsibility. We expect that at least 80% of participants
will identify gaps in their knowledge, particularly in areas related to detecting bias, algorithmic influence, and ethical content
sharing. To ensure continuous assessment and responsiveness, we will conduct daily reflection sessions, utilizing
structured qualitative feedback through group discussions, anonymous feedback forms, and real-time digital polling tools.
By analyzing the inputs, we aim to maintain a daily satisfaction score above 75% in terms of content relevance,
engagement, and clarity. If the satisfaction rate drops below this threshold, facilitators will implement adjustments to improve
delivery and engagement. A mid-mobility evaluation will assess participant satisfaction concerning logistical aspects (e.g.,
accommodation, food, and travel arrangements) as well as content delivery and facilitation effectiveness. This survey will
also include a mid-term knowledge quiz, assessing progress in misinformation detection, digital ethics, and fact-checking
skills. We expect participants to show at least a 30% improvement in quiz performance compared to baseline scores. If
scores fall below this target, facilitators will adapt session formats to enhance clarity and participant understanding. At the
end of the project, a final evaluation survey will measure overall knowledge acquisition, engagement, and satisfaction. This
will include a structured post-project knowledge test, where we aim for at least 80% of participants to demonstrate a
minimum 50% increase in correct answers compared to the baseline test. Additionally, at least 70% of participants should
self-report a significant improvement in their critical thinking and media literacy skills, which will be further validated through
their reflections in the Youthpass certificates awarded at the end of the project. To ensure an objective review of our
project’s success, two external evaluators with expertise in media literacy and project impact assessment will analyze the
survey data, participant feedback, and project outputs. Their role will be to validate whether the project has effectively
achieved its objectives and provide recommendations for improvement. They will also assess the reach and impact of the
project's dissemination activities, ensuring that the project outcomes extend beyond direct participants. The long-term
impact of the project will be measured through follow-up assessments at three and six months post-project. These
evaluations will track whether participants have applied their learning in real-world contexts. We aim for at least 50% of
participants to have taken active steps in combating misinformation, such as organizing at least one awareness event or
workshop in their local communities or online platforms. Furthermore, at least 30% of participants should demonstrate
tangible engagement, such as creating blog posts, videos or social media campaigns to promote media literacy. In addition
to participant actions, we will measure the digital reach and engagement of project outputs. Throughout the mobility,
participants will create at least 10 blog articles and 10 videos addressing misinformation-related challenges. These
materials will be shared widely across social media platforms, with a goal of reaching at least 2,000 unique users online. Our
key engagement metric (KPI) will be to achieve a minimum 10% engagement rate, meaning at least 200 interactions (likes,
shares, comments) across platforms. Analytics from Facebook, Instagram, and website traffic will be monitored to measure
audience reach, post visibility, and content impact. Additionally, we expect that participant-created resources (such as
articles and videos) will be viewed or downloaded at least 500 times within six months of project completion. To measure
the project's broader influence, we will track collaboration opportunities resulting from this initiative. We expect at least five
partner organizations to express interest in further working with us on misinformation-related projects, either by adopting
materials developed during this exchange or by initiating new joint initiatives. A real-time issue-tracking and risk
management plan will be in place throughout the mobility to resolve any arising challenges within 24-48 hours, ensuring
minimal disruption and maximum efficiency. Facilitators and project managers will maintain a problem-resolution KPI of
90%, meaning that the vast majority of logistical or content-related issues will be successfully addressed within this
timeframe.

relevant fields will be facilitated to guide participants in their ongoing initiatives. As Fiatalok Tere Egyesület is supporting us
at every level of the project lifetime, we will also make the results visible on their sites and social media platforms as well as
a physical dissemination activity. They will gain experience from the project and we will also discuss together the
development points and the details of the project.
How will you involve participants in such activities?
Active participant involvement is central to the success of our youth exchange. Through comprehensive digital media
literacy workshops, participants will gain critical skills to assess information, identify misinformation tactics and discern
credible sources. The core of the project involves collaborative efforts, with participants working in groups to create
impactful content, such as videos, infographics and social media campaigns, aimed at disseminating accurate information
and debunking prevalent myths. Actively engaging participants as digital ambassadors, they will share project-related
content on their personal social media platforms, extending the reach and impact of our initiative beyond the physical
confines of the exchange. Content creation challenges will incentivize creativity and sustained participation, with participants
contributing to the generation of compelling materials addressing various aspects of misinformation. Fostering a culture of
peer-to-peer learning, participants will share insights and success stories within the group, promoting a sense of collective
responsibility in addressing misinformation. Structured debates and discussions will provide a platform for participants to
voice their opinions and engage in constructive dialogue about the challenges posed by misinformation, encouraging critical
thinking and the exchange of diverse perspectives. Community outreach initiatives will see participants actively engaging
with local communities through workshops, seminars or awareness campaigns, extending the project's impact to a broader
audience. Furthermore, participants will contribute to the creation of educational resources, including informational guides,
fact- checking toolkits and instructional materials. This collaborative effort ensures that the project's outcomes can be
shared widely, contributing to broader digital literacy efforts. The created learning materials during the youth exchange will
support the dissemination activities, hence learning by doing will have a local impact as well. From the participants, by the
participants, for the participants and more. Regular feedback and reflection sessions will empower participants to actively
shape the trajectory of the project, sharing experiences and insights. This iterative feedback loop enhances the project's
effectiveness and ensures a lasting impact in the digital era. By integrating participants into diverse activities and providing
platforms for collaboration, creativity and reflection, our approach ensures their active involvement in combating
misinformation. This fosters a sense of ownership, empowerment and a lasting impact that extends well beyond the
immediate scope of the youth exchange initiative. People with fewer opportunities will get the necessary support to feel them
safe and welcoming after the project as well, hence they can continue their journey with our organizations even after the
project lifetime.
